items.title: Gag order lifted on identity of ex-soldier who murdered Nick Martin

children.content: The former soldier who assassinated ex-Rebels bikie boss Nick Martin at Perth Motorplex in 2020 can be publicly identified for the first time, after he successfully overturned a court order suppressing his identity.

children.content: Yamatji man Graham Taylor was just 19 when he shipped off to Vietnam. More than 50 years on, the brotherhood forged in the jungle of Vietnam remains solid.

items.title: 'We're not ready to leave': Inside the messy dynamic of a no-grounds eviction

children.content: Evicting a tenant without cause offers landlords a simple way out, but the issue can be much more complex than it appears. One such example of that complexity is Eleanor Kahlo's case of her no-grounds eviction.

items.title: Plan to end NDIS 'wild west' will flip rules on their head for providers

items.title: ASX 200 down after Wall Street losses, oil prices spike again — as it happened

children.content: Australia's ASX 200 has ended the week on a disappointing note, although the market clawed back most of its earlier losses to finish less than 0.1 per cent down for the day. Meanwhile, oil prices are rising again with Brent crude trading above $US105 a barrel. Look back on day's events with the ABC News markets blog.
